mnemonics.title: Vector Table First Two Entries
mnemonics.content: MSP First, Reset Second - Think 'MRS' (Main Stack, Reset) or remember: 0=M, 1=R (MSP, Reset)

mnemonics.title: System Exception Order
mnemonics.content: Remember: 'Rested N M H M B U S S P' - Reset, NMI, MemManage, HardFault, BusFault, UsageFault, SVCall, SysTick, PendSV

mnemonics.title: Vector Table Location
mnemonics.content: Zero-Zero-Zero (0x00000000) for CODE region, Eight-Zero-Zero (0x08000000) for FLASH - Both are 'power-of-2' aligned addresses.

acronyms.usage: Register in SCB used to relocate vector table to different memory address during runtime
Manages interrupt enable/disable, priority levels, and routes interrupts to handlers in vector table
Default stack pointer used by the processor, initialized from vector table entry 0
Alternative stack pointer used in Thread mode, selectable via CONTROL register
Contains system control registers including VTOR, AIRCR, and system exception configurations
External peripheral interrupt signals that connect to NVIC and are handled via vector table entries

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a simple array/list starting at address 0: Index0 has MSP value, Index1 has Reset_Handler, Index2 has NMI, and so on. Each entry is exactly 4 bytes (one word).
Think of the vector table as a 'phone directory' - when an event (call) occurs, the processor looks up the number (handler address) from the directory and dials (branches to) that handler.
Picture books being stacked when an exception arrives: PSR (top), PC, LR, R12, R3, R2, R1, R0 (bottom) - eight books pushed automatically by hardware.

analogies.analogy: Like a ship's emergency response plan - it lists exactly who handles each type of emergency (fire, medical, collision) and where to find them.
Like a forwarding address service - when you move, you tell the post office your new address; VTOR tells the processor where to find the new vector table.
Like a fire alarm system - the alarm triggers (exception), the system looks up the floor plan (vector table), and dispatches the correct response team (handler).
